key_insight: The paper learns how uncertainty changes with both context and decisions using nonparametric models, then plugs those learned residuals into a decision-dependent SAA framework for more accurate stochastic optimization.
engineering_relevance: This gives practitioners a data-driven way to model coupled prediction-and-decision problems, especially when uncertainty is not well described by a fixed parametric distribution.
method: Uses kNN, CART, and ReLU neural networks to predict decision- and context-dependent uncertainty.
Adds empirical residuals to predictions to form ER-DD-SAA and reduce model misspecification.
Derives exact MIP formulations; for kNN two-stage cases, proposes BD-CG (Benders + constraint generation).
evidence_signal: In numerical tests on a newsvendor-with-pricing problem and a two-stage facility location problem, the proposed nonparametric ER-DD-SAA outperforms a parametric benchmark out of sample and improves computational tractability.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the learned regression model and residual estimation, so poor coverage or noisy data can weaken the optimization guarantee in practice.
paper_title: Contextual Stochastic Optimization with Decision-Dependent Uncertainty via Nonparametric Learning
